event: involuntary guarding in the suprapubic region
spontaneous rupture of yellow purulent fluid expulsed from the posterior vaginal fornix
white blood cell count elevated at 18.7
transvaginal ultrasound revealed 5 cm × 2 cm complex collection in the posterior cul-de-sac
appendicitis not at the top of differential diagnosis
right ovarian torsion versus abscess formation due to appendicitis
antibiotic treatment with ceftriaxone and metronidazole
brought to operating room for diagnostic laparoscopy
1 × 1 cm defect in the posterior fornix of the vagina
continued expression of purulent, yellow fluid
right fallopian tube and ovary difficult to visualize
adhesions in the right lower quadrant
right lower quadrant Penrose drain placed
continued on piperacillin–tazobactam 3.375 g every 8 hours
oral cefpodoxime 200 mg orally twice a day for 7 days
final diagnosis: pelvic abscess due to acute, nonperforated appendicitis
endometriosis and decidualization found on pathological examination
uncomplicated normal spontaneous vaginal delivery at 40 1/7 weeks
